metadata.framework: Remote Applicant Guidelines for Global Workforce
metadata.description: Guidance for processing CVs from remote applicants regardless of current location or citizenship
metadata.context: Recognizes that remote work has globalized recruitment; agent should not assume EU-local citizenship

remote_applicant_definition.criteria: Any applicant applying for remote position from any global location
remote_applicant_definition.application: Use Europass as universal standard (internationally recognized)

location_transparency.requirement: Clearly state current location in CV
location_transparency.why: Employers need to understand time zones, visa requirements, and work location preferences
location_transparency.format: City, Country in Personal Information and/or Work Experience sections
examples: Remote (EU time zone preferred for collaboration)

timezone_considerations.where_to_include: Additional Information section or Personal Information
examples: Time zone: UTC+8; available for overlap with EU hours
Time zone: UTC-5; flexible for early morning meetings
Time zone: UTC-8; available Mon-Fri 2PM-10PM PT for PST-based team

scenarios.action: Disclose visa status in cover letter, not CV

scenarios.scenario: Job open to global candidates with sponsorship available
scenarios.action: Can note in 'Additional Information' or cover letter: 'Visa sponsorship eligible'

scenarios.scenario: Remote role with no location restriction
scenarios.action: No need to mention visa; CV remains location-neutral

work_authorization.right_to_work: EU applicants: natural 'right to work' in EU (mobility of labor)

non_eu_applicants.assumption: Do NOT assume applicant has work authorization in EU
non_eu_applicants.disclosure: If applying for EU-based roles, can note: 'Eligible for digital nomad visa' or 'Holding valid work permit'

relocation_intentions.employer_interest: Employers often want to know relocation willingness

disclosure_guidance.if_remote: Not necessary to declare relocation intent for remote roles

disclosure_guidance.if_hybrid: Can note in 'Additional Information': 'Available to relocate to [City] if opportunity requires'

disclosure_guidance.if_unclear: Cover letter is better place for relocation discussion than CV

international_experience.advantage: Remote applicants often bring cross-cultural perspective
highlighting_strategy: Include global client exposure if relevant
international_experience.example: Managed distributed team across 5 countries; experienced in async collaboration across time zones

language_multilingualism.remote_advantage: Remote roles often value multilingual capabilities
language_multilingualism.europass_cefr: Use CEFR scale (A1-C2) per Europass standards
language_multilingualism.ordering: Typically list by proficiency level (highest first) or by job relevance

communication_tools_proficiency.expectation: Remote roles require digital communication competency
tools_to_highlight: Synchronous communication (Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams)

contract_types.interpretation: Common in remote/freelance economy; not a red flag
Less concerning if explicit reason provided (upskilling, visa processing, etc.)
Can indicate career growth or remote opportunity-seeking

remote_work_evidence.how_to_demonstrate: Remote applicants should show history of remote work capability
evidence_inclusion: Online education or certifications (shows self-directed learning)
remote_work_evidence.example_role_description: Led cross-timezone development team (distributed across PST, GMT, IST); managed async standups and documentation-first development approach

remote_role_matching.cv_alignment: Structure CV to emphasize remote-relevant skills
remote_role_matching.integration: Extract these from work experience using CAR model

what_employers_assess.cv_reference: Current location and timezone in Personal Information
Previous remote roles, freelance experience, online education
Language proficiency, documentation examples, written communication evidence
International experience, long-term contracts, on-time delivery metrics

regional_preferences.guidance: This agent uses Europass as universal standard; no region-specific favoritism
regional_preferences.note: Europass is recognized in 70+ countries worldwide and accepted by international recruiters

data_privacy_for_remote_applicants.gdpr_applicability: GDPR applies if data is processed by EU-based employer
data_privacy_for_remote_applicants.international_applicant: CV may still be processed under GDPR if employer is EU-based
data_privacy_for_remote_applicants.data_minimization: Same rules apply regardless of applicant location

check_1.name: Location Transparency
check_1.rule: CV clearly states current location or 'Remote'

check_3.name: Remote Experience Evidence
check_3.rule: For remote roles, highlight prior remote work or distributed team experience

check_4.name: Communication Proficiency
check_4.rule: Digital skills and languages clearly listed

check_5.name: Employment Continuity Explanation
check_5.rule: If gaps or frequent changes, provide context

best_practices_for_remote_applicants: Clearly state current location and timezone
Highlight remote work experience or distributed team collaboration
Emphasize written communication and documentation skills
List relevant digital tools and platforms
Note language proficiencies with CEFR levels
If gap exists, briefly explain (relocations, visa processing, upskilling)
